intro.title: Hello, I'm Zhengbing He
intro.desc: Professor in Autonomous and Intelligent Systems, University of Nottingham Ningbo China. As a principal investigator, I have secured over 1 million US dollars in funding for national key research projects. I have published more than 180 academic papers, including over 50 papers published in top journals such as Transportation Research series and IEEE Transactions. I have published papers across all categories of Transportation Research (TR A/B/C/D/E/F), including more than 20 TRC papers and over 10 TITS papers, plus one Nature Correspondence. My academic indicators reach over 8000 total citations, i10-index above 100 and H-index over 40. I am an IEEE Senior Member and listed in the World’s Top 2% Scientists, ranking 67 out of 30588 researchers in Transportation & Logistics and 23536 across all fields. I serve as Senior Editor of IEEE Transactions on Intelligent Transportation Systems, Deputy Editor-in-Chief of IET Intelligent Transport Systems, Associate Editor of IEEE Transactions on Intelligent Vehicles, and Editorial Advisory Board Member of Transportation Research Part C. My research focuses on urban mobility, systems engineering and artificial intelligence, covering traditional fields including traffic flow operation and control, sustainability and resilience, as well as emerging directions such as data-driven modeling, autonomous driving and large language model applications. I am a pioneer researcher in AI-based transportation modeling and AV-enabled traffic congestion mitigation, and an early explorer of generative AI applications in transportation.

item0.title: Pioneer and long-term contributor to AI-based transportation modeling
item0.desc: Car-following and lane-changing models are core foundations for driver behavior analysis, traffic dynamics research and autonomous driving development. As early as 2014, in the early stage of deep learning development, my team proposed an innovative kNN-based data-driven car-following model, which has been cited nearly 200 times and widely recognized as one of the earliest data-driven car-following models by scholars from UC Berkeley, University of Washington and Imperial College London. In 2019, we developed the first DBN and LSTM based deep learning lane-changing model, gaining over 300 citations and being regarded as pioneering work in AI-driven traffic behavior modeling. In traffic prediction research, I developed the first CNN-based network-wide traffic speed prediction model with more than 1600 citations. Other representative works include spatio-temporal graph attention transformer trajectory prediction, graph CNN based shared mobility demand prediction and GAN-based travel time prediction. All related works are published in high-level journals including TRC, CACAIE and Information Fusion with great academic influence.

item1.title: An early innovator in large language model applications in transportation
item1.desc: Since LLMs became popular in 2023, my team has been among the first to explore LLM-based intelligent driving assistance, verified the effectiveness of prompt engineering in improving prediction accuracy, and built AI agent models for daily route planning, with a series of early studies published in top journals. These works belong to the earliest batch of LLM applications in transportation and have attracted widespread academic attention. In 2024, I was invited twice by SAE International to share research progress on LLM-enabled ground mobility. In early 2026, my team published a Nature Correspondence entitled "AI writing tools could lead scholars from low-income countries to erase their own voices".

item2.title: Developed autonomous driving strategies to mitigate traffic congestion and guide industry development
item2.desc: To solve highway congestion and emission problems, my team firstly proposed feedback control based congestion mitigation strategies and jam-absorption driving strategies for connected vehicles. Relevant achievements published in IEEE TITS have obtained more than 300 citations and been reported by overseas media. Japanese scholars cited our jam-absorption strategy many times and confirmed its practical effect on traffic relief. I am co-organizing a review paper with scholars from MIT, Georgia Tech and TU Delft to be published in IEEE TITS. For urban traffic scenarios, I proposed a novel lane-free autonomous driving traffic system and expanded it to pedestrian involved scenarios, with serial papers published in IEEE TIV, IEEE TVT and TRC and wide social attention. Many peer studies have affirmed the rationality of this innovative framework. I also focus on surrounding agent motion prediction for autonomous vehicles, especially intersection conflict scenarios, and have proposed prediction methods based on Transformer, CoT-enhanced LLM and World Models.

item3.title: Advanced traffic state sensing and visualization to boost practical applications
item3.desc: Time-space traffic diagram is a vital tool for traffic analysis and visualization. My team has conducted systematic research on diagram optimization, including mapping-to-cell methods, traffic wave-aware construction, traffic emission estimation and resolution improvement. Among them, the wave-aware construction method has been applied to the famous I-24 MOTION project in Nashville, and also adopted independently by research teams from the UK, Sweden and China.

item4.title: Also active in resilience, sustainability, shared mobility, and travel behavior
item4.desc: My research scope also covers transportation system dynamic vulnerability assessment, mesoscopic CO2 emission estimation considering traffic dynamics, multi-day ride-hailing travel behavior analysis and experimental route choice modeling, reflecting my comprehensive research capacity and team leadership ability. I have published multiple highly cited review papers in transportation fields, sorting out research progress of trajectory data analysis, automated vehicle related traffic research and transportation system resilience, each with more than 200 citations, providing clear research directions for peer scholars.

item5.title: Intelligent Rice Transplanting and Harvesting Robot
item5.desc: FreeRTOS-based agricultural robot designed for rice transplanting and harvesting. It adopts DJI 3508 motors to control gripper lifting mechanism and omnidirectional chassis, achieving positioning accuracy within ±5 mm. I participated in pneumatic structure design and hardware wiring, and realized dual-robot collaborative operation.

item6.title: SliverWolf Desktop Quadruped and Arm
item6.desc: An open-source desktop 12-DOF serial-link quadruped robot equipped with optional robotic arm and gripper. Based on STM32F103 and MG92R servos, the open-source repository contains 3D structure files, PCB documents, underlying firmware, voice control programs and various control demos. It supports standing, moving, turning, crawling, handshake, object fetching and other functions via Bluetooth, IMU, button and LD3320 voice control.

item7.title: 3508 Legged-Robot Drive Test Platform
item7.desc: A DJI M3508 driven quadruped robot verification platform iterated by MTI Robotics Team since 2022. Evolving from F427 ROBOCON chassis, it realizes remote control & keyboard control, adjustable PID parameters, stable standing, gyro balance, LiDAR height maintenance and complete gait control logic, which has passed multiple robot competition field tests.

item8.title: ROBOCON2024-PPS
item8.desc: MTIPPS is a planar base-axis positioning system developed for ROBOCON 2024 robots. Built with STM32F103C8T6, RS485 absolute encoders and omnidirectional wheel encoders, it realizes millimeter-level XY displacement recording and collects Hi219 IMU attitude data, transmitting real-time data to main controller for chassis positioning, path planning and autonomous driving tests.

item9.title: Typst Chinese Resume Template Library
item9.desc: A set of directly editable Typst resume templates supporting one-click PDF export. It includes various resume layouts, finished PDF files, preview images and matching avatar resources. Combined with VS Code and Tinymist extension, it supports real-time preview, CLI compilation, batch export and font configuration guidance.

item0.title: Robotics Engineer Learning Roadmap (Preview) - Lain-Ego on Zhihu
item0.desc: A systematic learning roadmap for robotics engineers, covering motion control, perception, embedded development and SLAM, with phased study plans and classified resource collection.

item1.title: A Guide to Career Paths in the Robotics Industry — for High School Students - KKR Database on Zhihu
item1.desc: An overview of robotics industry branches and career development directions, helping senior high school students match university majors with future career planning.

item2.title: On the Organizational Maturity of University Robotics Competition Teams - KKR Database on Zhihu
item2.desc: Discussions on team operation optimization, knowledge inheritance and collaborative management modes for university robot competition teams, to support long-term stable team development.

event11.title: Internship at Qianxing Intelligence
event11.desc: Responsible for humanoid robot motion control and human-robot interaction research and development.

event10.title: Launched LocoWiki Resource Hub
event10.desc: Built a resource platform for robot competition preparation and academic research, integrating competition rules, technical sharing, paper lists and high-quality open-source projects.

event12.title: Launched OpenDoge Open-Source Quadruped Crowdfunding
event12.desc: Served as core founding developer of OpenDoge open-source quadruped robot project, in charge of algorithm framework and underlying driver development. Official delivery is scheduled for late June 2026.

event1.title: Open-Sourced SliverWolf Desktop Serial-Link Quadruped
event1.desc: Released self-developed SliverWolf desktop serial-link quadruped robot with voice interaction and manipulator collaboration functions.

event2.title: 2024 ROBOCON National Competition
event2.desc: Developed FreeRTOS-based embedded control system for R1 robot, realizing ±5 mm high-precision positioning, ball launching and gripper grasping functions.

event4.title: 2024 ROBOCON National Competition
event4.desc: Developed FreeRTOS-based embedded control system for R1 robot, realizing ±5 mm high-precision positioning, ball launching and gripper grasping functions.

event5.title: Fujian Intelligent Marine Equipment Design Competition
event5.desc: Led control system development of buoyancy-adjustable bionic lobster underwater robot and won the grand prize.

event6.title: 2024 World Marine Equipment Conference
event6.desc: Attended exhibition on behalf of Fujian University of Technology to display bionic underwater robot, and the achievement was reported by Xinhua News Agency.

event7.title: 2025 ROBOCON National Competition
event7.desc: Completed full-stack research and development of legged robot, ranking 30th in speed race, 31st in obstacle race and 34th in cross-country race, winning national second prizes in all three events.

event8.title: 2025 National Undergraduate Electronics Design Contest
event8.desc: Completed hardware design and production of ranging system power detection module and won national second prize.

event9.title: Open-Sourced BRS Parallel-Link Quadruped Robot
event9.desc: Open-sourced full mechanical structure drawings and control code of self-designed BRS parallel-link quadruped robot on GitHub.

event13.title: Internship at HighTorque Robotics
event13.desc: Participated in mechanical design, control algorithm development and system joint debugging of HTDW4438 bionic quadruped robot, covering open-source prototype and commercial version iteration.
